description: Comprehensive Mimir/Cortex operational dashboard. Covers write path, read path, compactor, ruler, and resource utilization across clusters and stacks.

panels.description: Write-path SLO compliance: percentage of push requests completing within 500ms. Targets 99%+ for healthy operations.

targets.expr:
100 * (
  sum(rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~"/api/v1/push|api/prom/push", le="0.5"}[$__rate_interval]))
  /
  sum(rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~"/api/v1/push|api/prom/push", le="+Inf"}[$__rate_interval]))
)

panels.title: Write QoS

panels.description: Read-path SLO compliance: percentage of query requests completing within 1.0s. Targets 99%+ for healthy operations.

targets.expr:
100 * (
  sum(rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~".*query.*", le="1.0"}[$__rate_interval]))
  /
  sum(rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~".*query.*", le="+Inf"}[$__rate_interval]))
)

panels.title: Read QoS

panels.description: Total number of active series currently held in memory across all ingesters. A key capacity-planning metric for Mimir.
panels.title: Active Series

panels.description: Current ingestion rate in samples per second across all distributors. Reflects total write throughput into the Mimir cluster.
panels.title: Ingestion Rate

panels.description: Rate of rule evaluations per second across all ruler instances. Indicates how much recording/alerting rule processing load the cluster is handling.
panels.title: Rule Evaluations/s

panels.description: Number of compaction runs currently in progress (started minus completed). Non-zero values sustained over time may indicate compactor bottlenecks.
panels.title: Compactions Running

panels.description: Rate of samples received by distributors, broken down by cluster. Shows overall write throughput and helps identify load imbalances across clusters.
panels.title: Distributor — Samples Received/s

panels.description: Latency percentiles (P50, P90, P99) for push requests hitting the distributor. High P99 values may indicate tail-latency issues in the write path.
panels.title: Distributor — Request Latency

panels.description: Latency percentiles (P50, P99) for the ingester TSDB head appender. Measures the time it takes to append samples to the in-memory TSDB head block.
panels.title: Ingester — Append Latency

panels.description: Duration of WAL replay on ingester startup. Long replay times increase recovery time after restarts and may indicate oversized WAL segments.
panels.title: Ingester — WAL Replay Duration

panels.description: Rate of discarded samples broken down by reason (e.g., rate-limited, out-of-order, too-old). Helps identify systemic write-path issues and misconfigured clients.
panels.title: Write Failures by Type

panels.description: Rate of exemplars received by distributors. Exemplars link metrics to trace IDs, enabling seamless metrics-to-traces correlation.
panels.title: Distributor — Exemplars Received/s

panels.description: Queries per second hitting the query frontend, broken down by HTTP status code. 5xx responses indicate server-side failures requiring investigation.
panels.title: Query Frontend — QPS

panels.description: Latency percentiles (P50, P90, P99) for query frontend requests. Tracks end-to-end query latency as experienced by API consumers.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le) (rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~".*query.*"}[$__rate_interval])))
histogram_quantile(0.90, sum by (le) (rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~".*query.*"}[$__rate_interval])))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (rate(cortex_request_duration_seconds_bucket{cluster=~"$cluster", stack=~"$stack", namespace=~"$namespace", route=~".*query.*"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Query Frontend — Latency

panels.description: Rate of series fetched from store-gateways by queriers. High values may indicate expensive queries or missing caching layers.
panels.title: Querier — Series Fetched/s

panels.description: Current depth of the query scheduler queue. A growing queue indicates that queriers cannot keep up with incoming query load.
panels.title: Query Scheduler — Queue Length

panels.description: Number of TSDB blocks currently loaded by store-gateways. Correlates with memory usage and query performance for historical data.
panels.title: Store Gateway — Blocks Loaded

panels.description: Cache hit ratio across all Mimir cache layers (index, chunks, metadata, results). Low ratios indicate cold caches or insufficient cache capacity.
panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description: Rate at which the compactor cleans up blocks from object storage. Healthy compactors should show steady cleanup activity.
panels.title: Compaction Rate

panels.description: Rate of compaction failures. Persistent failures may lead to block accumulation, increased query latency, and eventually storage issues.
panels.title: Compaction Failures

panels.description: Total number of TSDB blocks in the object storage bucket per cluster. Growing block counts without corresponding compaction may indicate compactor issues.
panels.title: Bucket Size

panels.description: Rate of rule evaluations per second broken down by cluster. Shows recording and alerting rule processing throughput across the fleet.
panels.title: Rule Evaluations/s

panels.description: Rate of failed rule evaluations. Non-zero values indicate rules that cannot be evaluated, often due to query errors or resource constraints on the ruler.
panels.title: Rule Evaluation Failures

panels.description: CPU usage by Mimir component container. Identifies which components are CPU-bound and may need horizontal or vertical scaling.
panels.title: CPU by Component

panels.description: Working set memory usage by Mimir component container. Helps identify memory pressure and right-size resource requests/limits.
panels.title: Memory by Component

panels.description: Disk write throughput by Mimir component container. Critical for ingesters (WAL writes) and compactors (block compaction).
panels.title: Disk I/O

panels.description: Network receive and transmit throughput by pod. Helps identify network bottlenecks in inter-component communication (e.g., ingester replication, store-gateway fetches).
panels.title: Network I/O

title: Mimir Deep Dive — Multi-Stack Operations
